description:
You are acting as an MLOps engineer who needs to start tracking the very first set of
    experiment artifacts on a brand-new workstation.

    1.  A text file already exists at the absolute path  
            /home/user/sample_artifact.txt  
        This file is the model artefact produced by a training script.

    2.  Create the directory structure exactly as shown (create parent directories if they do
        not yet exist):

            /home/user/experiments/exp01/artifacts

    3.  Move (i.e. do NOT copy) the file
            /home/user/sample_artifact.txt  
        into the newly-created
            /home/user/experiments/exp01/artifacts/
        directory.  After this step the original path
            /home/user/sample_artifact.txt
        must no longer exist.

    4.  Produce a one-line CSV inventory file called  
            /home/user/experiments/exp01/exp01_artifacts.csv

        •  The file MUST contain exactly two lines:  
           a header line plus one data line.  
           No blank lines before, after or between them.

        •  The header line must be:
                artifact_name,size_bytes,sha256

        •  The second line must contain three comma-separated values in this order:
            1. artifact_name …… the base name of the artifact file (sample_artifact.txt)  
            2. size_bytes    …… the exact byte-length of the file  
            3. sha256        …… the full lowercase hexadecimal SHA-256 digest of the file

        •  A single newline character must terminate the second line (i.e. the
           file should end with “\n”).

        •  Do not include extra spaces, quotes, or additional columns.

    Hints (do NOT list the commands here): typical UNIX utilities such as
    “mkdir -p”, “mv”, “wc -c”, “sha256sum” or “cut/awk” are enough to achieve this
    in two or three simple shell commands.

    The automated checker will validate:

        a. The existence of /home/user/experiments/exp01/artifacts/sample_artifact.txt  
        b. The non-existence of /home/user/sample_artifact.txt  
        c. The exact two-line format of /home/user/experiments/exp01/exp01_artifacts.csv  
        d. That the size_bytes and sha256 values in the CSV match the actual file.

    Produce only the required directories, moved file and CSV.  No additional
    directories or files should be created.
truth:
<!--
        Pre-existing file required for the task
    -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/sample_artifact.txt">
            This is a sample artifact file for the experiment.
        </file>
    </initial_files>

    <!--
        Verification data – the test harness will recompute the hash/size from the file
        that ships with the container, so the exact numbers need not be hard-coded here.
        It is only important that the initial file has the above content and newline so
        that its size and SHA-256 are deterministic.
    -->
name: task_000000_842f22c5
